description: Parse chord symbols covering major, minor, and dominant seventh qualities
detail: A chord symbol is a root note (A–G, with optional b or # accidental) followed by an optional quality suffix. Three qualities are supported in the MVP: major (no suffix), minor ('m'), and dominant seventh ('7'). Examples: C, F#, Bb, Am, C#m, Bbm, G7, Bb7, F#7.
steps: Write a test that parses 'C' and asserts the AST node has root='C' and quality='major'
Write a test that parses 'F#' and asserts root='F#' and quality='major'
Write a test that parses 'Bb' and asserts root='Bb' and quality='major'
Write a test that parses 'Am' and asserts root='A' and quality='minor'
Write a test that parses 'C#m' and asserts root='C#' and quality='minor'
Write a test that parses 'G7' and asserts root='G' and quality='dominant7'
Write a test that parses 'Bb7' and asserts root='Bb' and quality='dominant7'
Write a test that asserts unsupported qualities (e.g. 'Cm7', 'CM7') are rejected by the parser

description: Parse a single bar containing one chord, delimited by single barlines
detail: A bar is written as | chord |. Only the single barline | is supported in the MVP. Each bar contains exactly one chord. Whitespace around the chord is insignificant.
steps: Write a test that parses '| C |' and asserts a bar node with a single chord C major
Write a test that parses '| Am |' and asserts a bar node with a single chord A minor
Write a test that parses '|C|' (no spaces) and produces the same AST as '| C |'
Write a test that parses '|  G7  |' (extra spaces) and produces the same AST as '| G7 |'
Write a test that asserts a bar with no chord (e.g. '| |') is rejected by the parser
Write a test that asserts a bar with two chords (e.g. '| C G |') is rejected by the parser

description: Parse a row of consecutive bars on a single line
detail: A row is a sequence of bars on one line, beginning and ending with a barline. The row produces an ordered list of bar nodes. The opening barline of the row and the closing barline are both represented in the AST.
steps: Write a test that parses '| C | Am | F | G |' and asserts four bar nodes in order
Write a test that parses a single-bar row '| C |' and asserts one bar node
Write a test that parses '| G7 | C | Am | F |' and asserts the chord qualities are correct for each bar
Write a test that asserts a row not starting with '|' is rejected by the parser
Write a test that asserts a row not ending with '|' is rejected by the parser

description: Parse YAML front matter containing title and key fields
detail: Front matter is a YAML block enclosed between '---' delimiters at the start of the file. Two fields are supported in the MVP: 'title' (a string) and 'key' (one of the 12 chromatic note names, with optional accidental: C, C#, Db, D, D#, Eb, E, F, F#, Gb, G, G#, Ab, A, A#, Bb, B). Both fields are optional.
steps: Write a test that parses a front matter block with title and key and asserts the correct values on the song AST node
Write a test that parses a front matter block with only a title (no key) and asserts key is null/undefined
Write a test that parses a front matter block with only a key (no title) and asserts title is null/undefined
Write a test that parses a source file with no front matter at all and asserts the song node has no title or key
Write a test that asserts an invalid key value (e.g. key: 'H') is rejected
Write a test that asserts an invalid key value with a mode (e.g. key: 'Am') is rejected (modes are not in scope for MVP)

description: Parse a complete song: optional front matter followed by one or more rows
detail: A complete .chart file consists of an optional YAML front matter block followed by one or more rows of bars. Blank lines between rows are permitted and ignored. The resulting AST is a song node containing metadata (from front matter) and an ordered list of rows.
steps: Write a test that parses a minimal song (no front matter, one row) and asserts the correct structure
Write a test that parses a song with front matter and multiple rows and asserts metadata and rows are both present
Write a test that parses a song with blank lines between rows and asserts blank lines do not produce extra row nodes

Parse each of the .chart files in packages/grigson/documentation/examples/ and assert that the parser does not throw (note: these files use features beyond MVP scope, so this step only asserts no crash on the supported subset)

description: Render a parsed song AST back to .chart plain text format
detail: The plain text renderer takes a song AST and produces a valid .chart string. The output is a normalised (pretty-printed) form: front matter fields each on their own line, each row of bars on its own line, single spaces around chord names inside bars. The output need not be byte-for-byte identical to the input, but it must parse back to an equivalent AST (round-trip equivalence).
steps: Write a test that renders a song with title and key and asserts the output contains a correctly formatted '---' YAML front matter block
Write a test that renders a single bar '| Am |' and asserts the output is '| Am |' with normalised spacing
Write a test that renders a row of four bars and asserts the output is a single line with all bars separated by barlines
Write a test that renders a multi-row song and asserts each row appears on its own line
Write a round-trip test: parse a source string, render to text, parse the rendered text, and assert the two ASTs are deeply equal
Run the round-trip test against each of the manually authored example songs that use only MVP-supported features

description: Install Prettier and format all non-generated source files
detail: Prettier is added as a devDependency and configured for the project. A pnpm script applies formatting to all files under packages/grigson/src/ except the generated parser output. The .gitignore or a .prettierignore file excludes generated files. All source files are formatted as part of this task.
steps: Install prettier as a devDependency
Add a .prettierrc config file with sensible defaults for a TypeScript project
Add a .prettierignore file excluding packages/grigson/src/parser/generated.js and packages/grigson/src/parser/generated.d.ts
Add a 'format' script to package.json that runs prettier --write on packages/grigson/src/
Run pnpm format and confirm it exits cleanly
Run pnpm test to confirm formatting did not break anything

description: Install ESLint with sensible configuration and fix any existing violations
detail: ESLint is added as a devDependency with a configuration appropriate for a TypeScript ESM project. A lint script is added to package.json. Any violations in existing source files are fixed before the task is marked complete. Generated parser files are excluded from linting.
steps: Install eslint and @eslint/js, typescript-eslint as devDependencies
Create an eslint.config.js (flat config) with rules appropriate for TypeScript and ESM
Exclude packages/grigson/src/parser/generated.js from linting
Add a 'lint' script to package.json that runs eslint on packages/grigson/src/
Run pnpm lint and fix all reported violations
Run pnpm test to confirm fixes did not break anything

description: Extend the parser to support the half-diminished chord quality (m7b5)
detail: The half-diminished quality (written as 'm7b5', e.g. Bm7b5) is needed to represent the ii° chord in harmonic minor keys (e.g. Bm7b5 | E7 | Am). The Quality union type gains a new 'halfDiminished' member. The Peggy grammar is extended to recognise the 'm7b5' suffix. The allowed-start-rules build script is unchanged.
steps: Write a test that parses 'Bm7b5' and asserts root='B' and quality='halfDiminished'
Write a test that parses 'Am7b5' and asserts root='A' and quality='halfDiminished'
Write a test that parses 'C#m7b5' and asserts root='C#' and quality='halfDiminished'
Write a test that the text renderer round-trips a chord with quality halfDiminished back to the 'm7b5' suffix
Add 'halfDiminished' to the Quality union type in packages/grigson/src/parser/types.ts
Extend the Peggy grammar in packages/grigson/src/parser/grammar.pegjs to match the 'm7b5' suffix and emit quality: 'halfDiminished'
Extend the TextRenderer to render halfDiminished chords with the 'm7b5' suffix
Run pnpm build to regenerate the parser, then run pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Implement the pitchClass theory module mapping note spellings to chromatic integers
detail: packages/grigson/src/theory/pitchClass.ts provides: (1) a PitchClass const object mapping canonical flat-side names to integers 0–11; (2) rootToPitchClass(root: string): PitchClass that accepts any valid spelling including enharmonic equivalents (e.g. 'A#' → 10); (3) ENHARMONIC_PAIRS mapping each sharp spelling to its flat equivalent and vice-versa (C#↔Db, D#↔Eb, F#↔Gb, G#↔Ab, A#↔Bb). Double accidentals (E#, B#, Cb, Fb) are out of scope.
steps: Write tests asserting rootToPitchClass returns the correct integer for every natural note: C=0, D=2, E=4, F=5, G=7, A=9, B=11
Write tests asserting rootToPitchClass handles all five sharp spellings: C#=1, D#=3, F#=6, G#=8, A#=10
Write tests asserting rootToPitchClass handles all five flat spellings: Db=1, Eb=3, Gb=6, Ab=8, Bb=10
Write a test asserting rootToPitchClass('A#') === rootToPitchClass('Bb') (enharmonic equivalence)
Write tests asserting ENHARMONIC_PAIRS maps 'A#' to 'Bb', 'Bb' to 'A#', and similarly for all five pairs
Write a test asserting rootToPitchClass throws (or returns undefined) for an unrecognised input like 'X'
Create packages/grigson/src/theory/pitchClass.ts and implement the PitchClass constants, ENHARMONIC_PAIRS, and rootToPitchClass
Run pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Implement the keys theory module defining diatonic note sets for all 24 in-scope keys
detail: packages/grigson/src/theory/keys.ts provides: (1) a KEYS record keyed by key name (e.g. 'F', 'Am') with each value containing the 7 diatonic note names as strings; (2) diatonicNotes(key: string): ReadonlySet<string> returning the note set for a given key. All 12 major keys and 10 harmonic minor keys are in scope (G#m and Ebm are excluded because they require double accidentals). The relative field linking each minor key to its relative major (and vice-versa) is also stored, to assist tie-breaking during key detection.
steps: Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('C') returns the set {C, D, E, F, G, A, B}
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('F') returns {F, G, A, Bb, C, D, E}
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('G') returns {G, A, B, C, D, E, F#}
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('Am') returns {A, B, C, D, E, F, G#} (harmonic minor with raised 7th)
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('Dm') returns {D, E, F, G, A, Bb, C#}

Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('F#') returns {F#, G#, A#, B, C#, D#, E#} — E# is in scope here as a diatonic note of F# major even though it is not a chord root that the normaliser will ever write
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes('Db') returns {Db, Eb, F, Gb, Ab, Bb, C}
Write a test asserting diatonicNotes for an unknown key throws or returns null
Write a test asserting KEYS contains exactly 22 entries (12 major + 10 minor)
Create packages/grigson/src/theory/keys.ts and implement KEYS and diatonicNotes
Run pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Key detector — Category 1: unambiguous major keys
detail: Create packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts and implement the basic scoring pass: for each of the 22 in-scope keys count how many chord roots (converted to pitch class via rootToPitchClass) are diatonic to that key; return the highest-scoring key name. This initial implementation does not yet need V7 weighting or tie-breaking — the Category 1 progressions are unambiguous enough to be decided by score alone.
steps: Write test T1-a: detectKey([F, Bb, C, F]) === 'F' (F major I-IV-V-I)
Write test T1-b: detectKey([G, D, Em, C]) === 'G' (G major I-V-vi-IV)
Write test T1-c: detectKey([Bb, Eb, F, Bb]) === 'Bb' (Bb major I-IV-V)
Write test T1-d: detectKey([E, A, B, E]) === 'E' (E major I-IV-V; B must not become Cb)
Write test T1-e: detectKey([F#, B, C#, F#]) === 'F#' (F# major I-IV-V)
Create packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts and implement detectKey with diatonic-root scoring
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1 tests pass

description: Key detector — Category 3: major/minor disambiguation
detail: Extend detectKey with relative-major/minor tie-breaking. When a major key and its relative harmonic minor are tied on diatonic score, apply (in order): (1) V7 of harmonic minor present → minor; (2) last-chord-as-tonic — if the last chord root matches a key's tonic, prefer that key; (3) first-chord-as-tonic (weaker); (4) prefer-major as final fallback. The half-diminished quality (Bm7b5) on the ii° degree is a strong minor signal.
steps: Write test T3-a: detectKey([Am, F, C, G7]) === 'C' (G7 = V7 of C major)
Write test T3-b: detectKey([Am, Dm, E7, Am]) === 'Am' (E7 = V7 of A harmonic minor)
Write test T3-c: detectKey([Am, F, C, G]) === 'C' (last chord G → C major via last-chord heuristic)
Write test T3-d: detectKey([C, Am, F, Am]) === 'Am' (last chord Am → A minor)
Write test T3-e: detectKey([Am, F, C, Am]) === 'Am' (first and last both Am)
Write test T3-f: detectKey([C, Am, F, Am]) === 'Am' (first C, last Am — last wins)
Write test T3-g: detectKey([Bm7b5, E7, Am, Am]) === 'Am' (ii° + V7 = strongest minor signal)
Extend packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts with V7/harmonic-minor detection and last/first-chord tiebreaking
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1 and Category 3 tests pass

description: Key detector — Category 4: V7 as a strong tonic anchor
detail: Extend detectKey so that a dominant-7th chord whose root is scale-degree V of a candidate key receives a heavy bonus (e.g. 5× the normal diatonic point), making V7 a near-definitive signal. This weight must be strong enough to resolve ties in Category 4 progressions but must not override a conclusive Category 1 majority.
steps: Write test T4-a: detectKey([Dm, G7, C]) === 'C' (ii-V-I; G7 = V7 of C)
Write test T4-b: detectKey([F, Bb, C7, F]) === 'F' (C7 = V7 of F major)
Write test T4-c: detectKey([D#, A#, F7, A#]) === 'Bb' (F7 = V7 of Bb; D#→Eb, A#→Bb after key confirmed)
Extend packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts to apply V7 dominant weighting during the scoring pass
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1, 3, and 4 tests pass

description: Key detector — Category 6: graceful degradation with sparse data
detail: Extend detectKey to handle sparse chord lists and the optional declaredKey parameter. Rules: (1) if declaredKey is among the top-scoring candidates, return it unchanged; (2) only override declaredKey if it scores zero (no diatonic overlap); (3) always return a key if any candidate scores > 0 — never return null when there is any match; (4) return null only when no chord root matches any candidate key.
steps: Write test T6-a: detectKey([C]) === 'C' (single chord, no declared key; best-guess C major)
Write test T6-b: detectKey([G, D], 'D') === 'D' (declared key D is a valid candidate — preserve it)
Write test T6-c: detectKey([G, D], 'E') === 'G' or 'D' but not 'E' (E scores zero — correct the declared key)
Write test T6-d: detectKey([F, Gm, C]) === 'F' (three chords uniquely identifying F major)
Extend packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts to accept and honour the declaredKey parameter
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1, 3, 4, and 6 tests pass

description: Key detector — Category 7: robustness to non-diatonic and chromatic chords
detail: Verify that detectKey handles borrowed and chromatic chord roots without being derailed. A single non-diatonic chord in an otherwise clear key should not change the result. A fully chromatic progression with no majority key should return null.
steps: Write test T7-a: detectKey([C, F, Ab, G]) === 'C' (Ab is chromatic/borrowed bVI; C F G anchor C major)
Write test T7-b: detectKey([C, A7, Dm, G7, C]) === 'C' (A7 is secondary dominant V7/ii; overall key C)
Write test T7-c: detectKey([C, Db, D, Eb]) === null (chromatic ascent — no key fits majority)
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1, 3, 4, 6, and 7 tests pass (no implementation changes expected)

description: Key detector — Category 9: enharmonic boundary keys (Db/C#, Gb/F#)
detail: Extend detectKey with an optional config parameter { fSharpOrGFlat?: 'f-sharp' | 'g-flat' } (default 'f-sharp'). For all keys except the F#/Gb pair, prefer the flat-side spelling when both enharmonic equivalents score equally. For F#/Gb specifically, use the config option to break the tie.
steps: Write test T9-a: detectKey([Db, Ab, Bbm, Gb]) === 'Db' (all chords diatonic to Db major; stay on flat side)
Write test T9-b: detectKey([Gb, Db, Ebm, Ab]) === 'Gb' (prefer flat spelling over F# major)
Write test T9-c default: detectKey([F#, C#, D#m, B]) === 'F#' (default fSharpOrGFlat = 'f-sharp')
Write test T9-c config: detectKey([F#, C#, D#m, B], null, { fSharpOrGFlat: 'g-flat' }) === 'Gb'
Extend packages/grigson/src/theory/keyDetector.ts to accept the config parameter and apply the F#/Gb tiebreak rule
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, and 9 tests pass

description: normaliseSong — Category 2: enharmonic correction of diatonic chord roots
detail: Create packages/grigson/src/theory/normalise.ts exporting normaliseSong(song: Song, config?: { fSharpOrGFlat?: 'f-sharp' | 'g-flat' }): Song. The initial implementation calls detectKey and rewrites every chord root that is diatonic to the detected key but spelled with the wrong enharmonic. Non-diatonic (borrowed) roots are left untouched. The function must be pure: it returns a new Song and never mutates its input.
steps: Write test T2-a: | F | A# | F | C | → A# rewritten to Bb; key detected as F
Write test T2-b: | Bb | D# | F | Bb | → D# rewritten to Eb; key detected as Bb
Write test T2-c: two rows each containing A# in F major — both instances rewritten to Bb
Write test T2-d: | F | A# | F | Bb | — A# rewritten to Bb, existing Bb unchanged
Write test T2-e: | C# | F# | G# | C# | → all three roots rewritten to Db Gb Ab Db; key detected as Db
Write test T2-f: | D# | A# | F | D# | → D#→Eb, A#→Bb; key detected as Bb
Write a test asserting normaliseSong returns a new Song object (not the same reference as the input)
Write a test asserting normaliseSong is idempotent: calling it twice produces the same output as calling it once
Create packages/grigson/src/theory/normalise.ts and implement normaliseSong with diatonic-root enharmonic rewriting
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 2 tests pass

description: normaliseSong — Category 5: front matter key field read and write
detail: Extend normaliseSong so that after detecting the canonical key it updates the song's front matter: if the key field is already correct, leave it; if it is wrong (i.e. not among the top-scoring candidates), replace it; if it is absent, add it. The chord roots are also corrected as in Cat 2.
steps: Write test T5-a: song with key: F and chords | F | Bb | C | F | — key field unchanged, no chord changes
Write test T5-b: song with key: G and chords | F | Bb | C | F | — key field corrected to F
Write test T5-c: song with no front matter and chords | G | D | Em | C | — key: G added to front matter
Write test T5-d: song with key: C and chords | Bb | F | C | Gm | — key corrected to F (Bb and Gm not diatonic to C)
Extend normaliseSong to read and write the song's key field in front matter
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 2 and Category 5 tests pass

description: normaliseSong — Category 8: borrowed chords and secondary dominants
detail: Extend normaliseSong with a next-chord borrowing heuristic for non-diatonic roots: assume the chord is a secondary dominant (V or V7) and look at the following chord to determine the correct spelling. When the heuristic is inconclusive, fall back to preferring the flat spelling for pitch classes 1, 3, 6, 8, 10. Chord roots that are already correctly spelled must not be disturbed.
steps: Write test T8-a: | F | Bb | Eb7 | Ab | — Eb7 is V7 of Ab (next chord); spelling Eb7 is correct (not D#7)
Write test T8-b: | C | A7 | Dm | G7 | C | — A7 root stays A (diatonic to C major as vi); no root rewrite
Write test T8-c: | C | Bb | F | C | — Bb is borrowed bVII in C major; stays Bb (not A#); key stays C
Write test T8-d: | C | G | Ab | Bb | C | — Ab and Bb are borrowed bVI/bVII in C; both spellings preserved; key stays C
Extend packages/grigson/src/theory/normalise.ts with next-chord borrowing analysis and flat-fallback for non-diatonic roots
Run pnpm test to confirm all Category 2, 5, and 8 tests pass

description: Set up the CLI entry point, shebang, and subcommand dispatch
detail: Create packages/grigson/src/cli.ts as the CLI entry point with a Node.js shebang. Add a 'bin' field to packages/grigson/package.json pointing to packages/grigson/dist/cli.js. Install a lightweight argument-parsing library. Implement top-level subcommand dispatch and --help output. Unknown subcommands should exit with a non-zero code and a useful error message.
steps: Install a lightweight argument-parsing library (e.g. minimist or commander) as a dependency
Create packages/grigson/src/cli.ts with a #!/usr/bin/env node shebang and top-level subcommand dispatch
Add a 'bin' field to packages/grigson/package.json: { "grigson": "./dist/cli.js" }
Ensure the TypeScript build includes packages/grigson/src/cli.ts and emits packages/grigson/dist/cli.js
Implement --help / -h output that lists available subcommands and exits with code 0
Write a test asserting that invoking the CLI with an unknown subcommand exits with a non-zero code
Write a test asserting that invoking the CLI with --help exits with code 0 and prints usage
Run pnpm build and confirm packages/grigson/dist/cli.js is produced with the shebang line intact

description: Implement the `grigson normalise` subcommand
detail: The normalise subcommand reads a .chart file (from a file argument or stdin), runs normaliseSong, and writes the result to stdout. Supports --key to override key detection, --enharmonic to control F#/Gb preference, and -i/--in-place to edit the file on disk instead of writing to stdout.
steps: Write a test asserting that `grigson normalise file.chart` writes normalised .chart text to stdout
Write a test asserting that `grigson normalise` with no file argument reads from stdin
Write a test asserting that --key <key> overrides key detection (e.g. --key Am)
Write a test asserting that --enharmonic g-flat is forwarded to normaliseSong config
Write a test asserting that -i edits the file in place and produces no stdout
Write a test asserting that -i with no file argument exits with a non-zero code and an error message
Implement the normalise subcommand handler in packages/grigson/src/cli.ts using parseSong, normaliseSong, and TextRenderer
Run pnpm build && pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Implement the `grigson render` subcommand for plain-text output
detail: The render subcommand reads a .chart file (from a file argument or stdin), parses it, and writes the rendered output to stdout. The only supported format in this task is 'text' (the default). An unknown --format value should exit with a non-zero code. Unlike normalise, render does not run normaliseSong — it is a pure parse-and-render operation.
steps: Write a test asserting that `grigson render file.chart` writes plain-text .chart to stdout
Write a test asserting that `grigson render` with no file argument reads from stdin
Write a test asserting that `grigson render --format text` behaves identically to the default
Write a test asserting that `grigson render --format svg` exits with a non-zero code and a 'not yet implemented' message
Write a test asserting that `grigson render --format unknown` exits with a non-zero code
Implement the render subcommand handler in packages/grigson/src/cli.ts using parseSong and TextRenderer
Run pnpm build && pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Implement the `grigson transpose` subcommand
detail: The transpose subcommand reads a .chart file (from a file argument or stdin), transposes all chords by the specified interval, updates the key field in front matter, and writes the result to stdout. Accepts either --semitones <n> (positive or negative integer) or --to <key>; exactly one must be provided. This task is blocked until the grigson library exports a transposeSong function.
steps: Write a test asserting that `grigson transpose --semitones 2 file.chart` transposes all chords up a whole step
Write a test asserting that `grigson transpose --semitones -3 file.chart` transposes down a minor third
Write a test asserting that `grigson transpose --to G file.chart` transposes to G major
Write a test asserting that providing both --semitones and --to exits with a non-zero code
Write a test asserting that providing neither --semitones nor --to exits with a non-zero code
Write a test asserting that `grigson transpose` with no file argument reads from stdin
Implement the transpose subcommand handler in packages/grigson/src/cli.ts using the (not yet available) transposeSong export
Run pnpm build && pnpm test to confirm all tests pass

description: Set up the Eleventy website sub-project structure
detail: Create a packages/website/ directory as a self-contained Eleventy project with its own package.json and node_modules. Install @11ty/eleventy as a dev dependency. Add an .eleventy.js config that reads from packages/website/content/, uses packages/website/_includes/ for layouts, and writes to packages/website/_site/. Add a base Nunjucks layout at packages/website/_includes/base.njk with minimal HTML shell (head, nav, main, footer). Add packages/website/_site/ and packages/website/node_modules/ to the root .gitignore. Confirm that running pnpm run build inside packages/website/ produces the _site/ directory without errors. See project/plans/mossy-honking-star.md for the full design.
steps: Create packages/website/package.json with name, version, scripts (build: eleventy, serve: eleventy --serve), and @11ty/eleventy as a devDependency
Create packages/website/.eleventy.js with addPassthroughCopy('assets') and dir config: { input: 'content', includes: '../_includes', output: '_site' }
Create packages/website/_includes/base.njk with a minimal HTML layout: doctype, head with charset/viewport/title, a nav placeholder, a <main> block for {{ content | safe }}, and a footer
Add packages/website/_site/ and packages/website/node_modules/ to the root .gitignore
Run pnpm install inside packages/website/ and confirm it succeeds
Create a temporary packages/website/content/index.md with front matter (layout: base.njk, title: grigson) and placeholder body text
Run pnpm run build inside packages/website/ and confirm _site/index.html is produced
Remove the temporary index.md (it will be added properly in website-content)

description: Add documentation content pages to the Eleventy website
detail: Create packages/website/content/ with five pages: an index.md homepage and four documentation pages derived from the existing documentation/ markdown files. Each page needs Eleventy front matter (layout, title, permalink). The documentation content should be adapted for the web (add front matter, fix relative links if any). Confirm that pnpm run build inside packages/website/ renders all five pages to _site/. See project/plans/mossy-honking-star.md for the full design.
steps: Create packages/website/content/index.md: front matter with layout: base.njk, title: grigson, permalink: /; body with a short description of grigson and links to the documentation pages
Create packages/website/content/format.md: front matter with layout: base.njk, title: Chart Format, permalink: /format/; body copied from packages/grigson/documentation/README.md
Create packages/website/content/cli.md: front matter with layout: base.njk, title: CLI Reference, permalink: /cli/; body copied from packages/grigson/documentation/cli.md
Create packages/website/content/renderer.md: front matter with layout: base.njk, title: Renderer, permalink: /renderer/; body copied from packages/grigson/documentation/renderer.md
Create packages/website/content/testing.md: front matter with layout: base.njk, title: Testing, permalink: /testing/; body copied from packages/grigson/documentation/testing.md
Update packages/website/_includes/base.njk nav to include links to all five pages
Run pnpm run build inside packages/website/ and confirm _site/ contains index.html, format/index.html, cli/index.html, renderer/index.html, testing/index.html

description: Add GitHub Actions workflow to deploy the website to GitHub Pages
detail: Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml with two jobs: build (checkout, setup-node, pnpm install in packages/website/, pnpm run build in packages/website/, upload-pages-artifact from packages/website/_site/) and deploy (deploy-pages). The workflow triggers on push to main. Required permissions: contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write. Note: the GitHub repository must be configured with Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions for this to work. See project/plans/mossy-honking-star.md for the full design.
steps: Create .github/workflows/ directory if it does not exist
Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml with the workflow trigger (push to main), permissions block, build job, and deploy job as specified in the plan
Confirm the YAML file is valid (no syntax errors) by running: node -e "require('js-yaml').load(require('fs').readFileSync('.github/workflows/deploy.yml','utf8'))" or similar
Commit the workflow file

description: Set up a pnpm workspace so website can depend on grigson
detail: Add a pnpm-workspace.yaml at the repo root listing 'packages/*' as packages. Update packages/website/package.json to declare grigson as a dependency using the workspace:* protocol. Run pnpm install from the root to verify the workspace links correctly and that importing from 'grigson' resolves inside packages/website/node_modules.
steps: Create pnpm-workspace.yaml at the repo root with packages: ['packages/*']
Add 'grigson': 'workspace:*' to the dependencies field of packages/website/package.json
Run pnpm install from the repo root and confirm it exits cleanly
Confirm that packages/website/node_modules/grigson is a symlink pointing to packages/grigson

description: Configure Vite library mode in grigson to produce a browser bundle
detail: Add Vite as a devDependency to the grigson package. Create packages/grigson/src/index.browser.ts as the browser entry point, exporting only the browser-safe API (parseSong, parseChord, TextRenderer — no CLI code). Add packages/grigson/vite.config.ts configured for library mode, producing packages/grigson/dist/grigson.iife.js (global name 'grigson') and packages/grigson/dist/grigson.esm.js. Add a 'build:browser' script to packages/grigson/package.json and incorporate it into the main 'build' script. Confirm the IIFE bundle exposes grigson.parseSong and grigson.TextRenderer as globals.
steps: Install vite as a devDependency in the grigson package
Create packages/grigson/src/index.browser.ts exporting parseSong, parseChord, and TextRenderer (no CLI imports)
Create packages/grigson/vite.config.ts in library mode: entry src/index.browser.ts, formats ['iife', 'es'], fileName 'grigson', globalName 'grigson', output to dist/
Add a 'build:browser' script to packages/grigson/package.json that runs 'vite build'
Add 'build:browser' as the final step in the main 'build' script
Run pnpm build and confirm packages/grigson/dist/grigson.iife.js and packages/grigson/dist/grigson.esm.js are produced
Verify the IIFE bundle exports grigson.parseSong and grigson.TextRenderer (e.g. via node --input-type=module or a quick smoke test)

description: Wire the grigson browser bundle into the Eleventy website
detail: Configure Eleventy to copy the grigson IIFE bundle from the workspace into the website's _site/js/ directory. Add a <script> tag to packages/website/_includes/base.njk that loads /js/grigson.iife.js, making the grigson global available on every page. The copy should source from packages/website/node_modules/grigson/dist/ (resolved via the pnpm workspace symlink) so it stays in sync with the built library.
steps: In packages/website/.eleventy.js, add an addPassthroughCopy mapping node_modules/grigson/dist/grigson.iife.js to js/grigson.iife.js
Add <script src='/js/grigson.iife.js'></script> to the <head> (or end of <body>) in packages/website/_includes/base.njk
Run pnpm build in the root (to ensure packages/grigson/dist/grigson.iife.js exists), then pnpm build in packages/website/
Confirm packages/website/_site/js/grigson.iife.js is present in the built output

description: Add a page showing a before/after normalisation example powered by grigson
detail: Create packages/website/content/demo.md (permalink: /demo/) with a hardcoded 'badly formatted' chart example. An inline <script> on the page calls grigson.parseSong() and grigson.normaliseSong() at page load, then writes the normalised output into a <pre> element. The input is shown as static text; the output is injected by the script. This validates that the browser bundle works end-to-end on a real page.
steps: Add normaliseSong to the exports in packages/grigson/src/index.browser.ts and rebuild the browser bundle
Create packages/website/content/demo.md with layout: base.njk, title: Demo, permalink: /demo/
Add a hardcoded input chart string, a <pre id='input'> block showing it, and a <pre id='output'> block as the target
Add an inline <script> that calls grigson.parseSong() + grigson.normaliseSong() + grigson.TextRenderer and sets output.textContent
Add a link to /demo/ in the nav in packages/website/_includes/base.njk
Run pnpm build in packages/website/ and open _site/demo/index.html to confirm the output is rendered correctly

description: Add an interactive playground page with live normalisation output
detail: Create packages/website/content/playground.md (permalink: /playground/) with a <textarea> for chart input and a <pre> for normalised output. An inline <script> listens to input events on the textarea, calls grigson.parseSong() + grigson.normaliseSong() + TextRenderer on each keystroke, and updates the output element. Parse errors should be caught and displayed as a plain error message rather than crashing the page.
steps: Create packages/website/content/playground.md with layout: base.njk, title: Playground, permalink: /playground/
Add a <textarea id='input'> with a placeholder chart and a <pre id='output'> for results
Add an inline <script> with an 'input' event listener that calls parseSong → normaliseSong → TextRenderer.render and updates output.textContent
Wrap the processing in a try/catch and display error.message in the output element on parse failure
Pre-populate the textarea with a short example chart containing at least one enharmonic spelling that normalisation will correct
Add a link to /playground/ in the nav in packages/website/_includes/base.njk
Run pnpm build in packages/website/ and confirm the page builds; manually verify in a browser that typing updates the output live

description: Complete the TextMate grammar to cover all Grigson syntax
detail: Update the TextMate grammar in packages/textmate-grammar/grigson.tmLanguage.json to support all syntax elements defined in the Peggy grammar: front matter delimiters and fields, chord roots and accidentals, and all quality suffixes (m, 7, m7b5). The vscode-extension's copy should be kept in sync.
steps: Add frontmatter repository item with patterns for '---' delimiters and 'key:', 'title:' fields
Add chord repository item with patterns for NoteLetter (A-G), Accidental (#, b), and Quality suffixes (m, 7, m7b5)
Update the main patterns to include frontmatter, barline, and chord
Copy the updated grammar to packages/vscode-extension/syntaxes/grigson.tmLanguage.json
Create a sample packages/grigson/documentation/examples/syntax-test.chart using all features and verify highlighting

description: Integrate Shiki for highlighted output in the playground
detail: Use the Shiki library to apply syntax highlighting to the normalised output in the playground. The highlighter should use the Grigson TextMate grammar to ensure consistent styling with VS Code.
steps: Install shiki as a devDependency in packages/website
Configure Eleventy to copy packages/textmate-grammar/grigson.tmLanguage.json to _site/js/grigson.tmLanguage.json
Update packages/website/content/playground.md to load Shiki and register the Grigson language
Update the playground update() function to use shiki.codeToHtml for the output element
Add basic CSS for Shiki tokens to ensure the highlighted code is legible

description: Replace textarea with Monaco Editor in the playground
detail: Improve the input experience in the playground by replacing the basic <textarea> with a Monaco Editor instance. This provides a richer editing environment and sets the stage for grammar-based highlighting.
steps: Install monaco-editor as a devDependency in packages/website
Configure Eleventy to copy Monaco workers and necessary assets to _site/js/monaco/
Update packages/website/content/playground.md to replace <textarea> with a <div> container
Initialize Monaco Editor in the playground script with the default chart content
Link Monaco's onDidChangeModelContent to the live-normalisation update() logic

description: Enable TextMate-based highlighting in Monaco Editor
detail: Use monaco-textmate and vscode-oniguruma to provide full syntax highlighting in the Monaco input editor using the Grigson TextMate grammar.
steps: Install monaco-textmate and vscode-oniguruma as devDependencies in packages/website
Configure the playground script to initialize vscode-oniguruma with its WASM binary
Register the Grigson language in Monaco and provide a TextMate grammar provider
Verify that typing in the Monaco editor now shows full syntax highlighting matching the Shiki output

description: Scaffold the side-effect-free custom element and build pipeline
detail: Create the GrigsonChart class and its registration entry point. Update the build pipeline to produce an auto-registering bundle.
steps: Create packages/grigson/src/element.ts with a side-effect-free GrigsonChart class extending HTMLElement
Create packages/grigson/src/register.ts to call customElements.define('grigson-chart', GrigsonChart)
Export GrigsonChart from packages/grigson/src/index.browser.ts
Update packages/grigson/vite.config.ts to produce a grigson-register.iife.js bundle from src/register.ts
Run pnpm build and verify both the core and register bundles are produced

description: Implement basic chart parsing and rendering in the custom element
detail: The custom element should find a child <template>, parse its chart content, and render it to a Shadow Root using the TextRenderer.
steps: Implement connectedCallback in GrigsonChart to locate a child <template> and parse its innerHTML
Initialize a Shadow Root and render the parsed song to a <pre> element
Implement an update() method to handle re-rendering
Verify that <grigson-chart><template>| C |</template></grigson-chart> renders correctly in a browser

description: Enable attribute-based configuration and reactivity
detail: Map custom element attributes like transpose-key and notation-preset to renderer configuration and trigger re-renders on change.
steps: Define observedAttributes for renderer, transpose-key, transpose-semitones, and notation-preset
Implement attributeChangedCallback to update the internal config and call update()
Verify that changing attributes via the DOM (e.g., setAttribute) updates the rendered chart

description: Enable declarative configuration via child elements
detail: Allow configuration using child elements like <grigson-transpose> and use a MutationObserver to react to changes.
steps: Implement a MutationObserver in GrigsonChart to watch for additions/removals/changes to child config elements
Implement logic to harvest config from <grigson-transpose>, <grigson-notation>, etc.
Verify that adding or modifying child elements triggers a re-render with the new configuration

description: Implement a CSS API with Custom Properties, Parts, and Theme Support
detail: Provide a default stylesheet in the Shadow DOM that supports light/dark mode and exposes variables and parts for user customisation.
steps: Add a default CSS block to the Shadow Root using CSS Custom Properties for colors and fonts
Add part='...' attributes to internal elements (chord-root, barline, etc.)
Implement prefers-color-scheme media queries in the default styles
Verify that the element respects system dark mode and can be styled via ::part() and CSS variables

description: Integrate the custom element into the website and playground
detail: Replace manual scripting on the website with the new <grigson-chart> element to verify real-world usage.
steps: Update packages/website/.eleventy.js to passthrough the new grigson-register.iife.js bundle
Update packages/website/_includes/base.njk to load the register bundle
Refactor demo.md and playground.md to use <grigson-chart> instead of manual grigson.parseSong calls
Verify the website documentation and interactive features still function correctly
